    • Modified Weibull Distribution (MWD)

Modified Weibull Distribution (MWD)

Source: vignettes/mwd-distribution.Rmd
mwd-distribution.Rmd

MWD Distribution

The modified Weibull distribution (MWD), introduced by Lai et al. (2003), which has been widely used in reliability and survival analysis. A random variable XX is said to follow a modified Weibull distribution if its cumulative distribution function (cdf) F(x)F(x) and probability density function (pdf) f(x)f(x) are given by F(x)=1−exp(−axbeλx),F(x) = 1- \exp \big( -a x^b e^{\lambda x} \big), and f(x)=a(b+λx)xb−1eλxexp(−axbeλx),f(x) = a (b + \lambda x) x^{b-1} e^{\lambda x} \exp \big( -a x^b e^{\lambda x} \big), where x>0x>0, a>0a>0 is the scale parameter, b≥0b \ge 0 is a shape parameter, and λ≥0\lambda \ge 0 is an acceleration or flexibility parameter that controls how quickly the hazard grows over time. Then, the hazard function is h(x)=a(b+λx)xb−1eλx. h(x) = a (b + \lambda x) x^{b-1} e^{\lambda x}. When λ=0\lambda=0, it reduces to the two-parameter Weibull distribution with F(x)=1−exp(−axb)F(x) = 1- \exp(-a x^b). When b=0b=0, it reduces to a type I extreme-value (or log-gamma) distribution with F(x)=1−exp(−aeλx)F(x) = 1- \exp(-a e^{\lambda x} ).

References

Lai, C. D., Xie, M., & Murthy, D. N. P. (2003). A modified Weibull distribution. IEEE Transactions on Reliability, 52(1), 33–37. doi.org/10.1109/TR.2002.805788
